title: Logo Vectorization + Brand Identity Kit — HaulAway (junk removal / vehicle-wrap-ready)
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the source brief: client = "HaulAway", a junk removal / cleanout company in Maitland, FL (North Orlando); they have an existing raster logo they love and need it vectorized + cleaned to print-ready quality; required deliverables explicitly listed = master logo in vector (AI/EPS/SVG + high-res PNG), horizontal layout, stacked/vertical layout, icon-only "AB mark" version, one-color version (white-on-dark / black-on-light), brand color sheet with hex/CMYK/Pantone, font identification + usage guide, and files sized for t-shirts / vehicle+trailer wrap / car magnets / print; brand palette = black, lime green, gray; "bold, clean, professional"; client invites a creative bonus variation that would "pop on a trailer wrap or shirt"; client explicitly rejects JPG/PNG-only deliverables and demands real vector work. REASONABLY ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe-connector benchmark: the supplied logo is a flat low-res PNG with a transparent-ish background (typical "logo I have but only as a JPG/PNG" situation); I fixed concrete numbers for the chain (vectorize the cleaned master, isolate the "HA" monogram with select_by_prompt→invert→solid-fill before re-vectorizing the icon, derive the one-color/knockout versions via solid fill + color-overlay, crop the horizontal and stacked compositions to bounds, and recolor a lime-on-black "trailer wrap" hero variation). The hex/CMYK/Pantone "color sheet", font-usage guide, and presentation are delivered as a font_recommend report + a Firefly Board of the produced assets, NOT as a from-scratch composed page (layout composition is out of connector scope). Tool selection, ordering, exec-mode tags, and the white-on-dark / black-on-light knockouts are enriched but faithful to the listed deliverables — no unrelated deliverable invented.
one_line_ask: Vectorize and clean up HaulAway's existing raster logo to print-ready quality, then build the full set of brand variations (master vector, horizontal, stacked, icon-only HA mark, one-color knockouts, a lime-on-black trailer-wrap hero) plus a font + color-usage reference and a presentation board — all wrap/shirt/print ready.
full_brief:
HaulAway (junk removal & cleanout, Maitland FL / North Orlando) supplies ONE existing logo they love but only as a flat, low-resolution raster PNG (a bold "HA" monogram lockup with the company wordmark beneath, in their black / lime-green / gray palette). They need it turned into a professional, print-ready brand identity kit usable on t-shirts, vehicle & trailer wraps, car magnets, and print.

The freelancer must drive the Adobe Creative Cloud connector end-to-end:
1) Ingest the client's raster logo and get a clean working copy (auto-tone + straighten the slightly skewed scan so edges are true).
2) Clean the color: neutralize any off-cast and crisp up the lime green and the blacks so the trace is sharp.
3) Clean-knock the background to transparent so the trace has no halo.
4) Vectorize the cleaned full lockup into a clean SVG = the MASTER vector (basis for AI/EPS/SVG/PNG exports).
5) Isolate the "HA" monogram for the ICON-ONLY mark: select the wordmark text by prompt, invert to keep just the monogram, solid-fill the wordmark region away with a solid white fill, then vectorize the isolated mark.
6) Produce the ONE-COLOR versions: a black-on-light knockout (solid black overlay) and a white-on-dark knockout (white fill) of the mark.
7) Build the layout LOCKUPS by cropping the cleaned master to its true bounds for the horizontal version and a tighter square for the stacked/vertical and the magnet.
8) Create the creative bonus "trailer-wrap hero": recolor the mark to a high-impact lime treatment, then set the surrounding canvas to solid black so the mark reads lime-on-black across a trailer side.
9) Identify/recommend the brand font family + a usage pairing (font_recommend) as the typography guide.
10) Assemble all produced variations into a Firefly Board as the client-facing presentation / brand sheet (carrying the hex/CMYK/Pantone callouts in the board notes).

Hard rules honored: ABSOLUTELY no generative fill, no AI object removal, no text-to-image, no compositing, no layout composition; image_fill_area is solid-color only; the master export step only EXPORTS genuine vector — the deliverable vector emerges from image_vectorize, not from an authored .ai. Every step after ingest consumes a prior step's output.

inputs.name: haulaway_logo_raw.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat, slightly low-resolution company logo PNG for a junk-removal business called 'HaulAway'. A bold geometric 'HA' monogram mark in lime green (#9ACD32) and black sits above a clean sans-serif wordmark reading 'HaulAway' with a thin gray tagline 'HAULING • CLEANOUTS • DEMOLITION' beneath it. Black, lime-green and gray palette only. The whole logo is very slightly rotated about 2.5 degrees clockwise (as if scanned off a business card), set on an almost-white #F4F4F2 background with faint JPEG-style compression softness around the edges. Crisp readable type, no drop shadows, no gradients. Realistic flat-art logo file, not glossy 3D.
inputs.realism_notes: Must read as a real client-supplied 'I only have this as a PNG' asset: mild compression softness, a true ~2.5° skew so auto_straighten has work to do, near-white (not pure-white) background so remove_background and a color cleanup are warranted, and crisp enough type that vectorize yields clean paths. Text must be legible so select_by_prompt can target the 'HaulAway' wordmark vs the 'HA' monogram.

outputs.name: haulaway_master_logo.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vectorized master lockup (full HA monogram + wordmark + tagline) as scalable SVG with crisp paths, transparent background; the print-ready master from which AI/EPS/PNG exports derive. Black/lime/gray palette preserved.

outputs.name: haulaway_icon_mark.svg
outputs.spec: Icon-only 'HA' monogram, isolated (wordmark removed via select→invert→solid-white-fill) and vectorized; transparent background; for app icons, magnets, embroidery.

outputs.name: haulaway_mark_oneclr_black_on_light.png
outputs.spec: One-color knockout: solid black HA mark on transparent/light, hi-res PNG for single-color print, stamps, light-shirt screen-print.

outputs.name: haulaway_mark_oneclr_white_on_dark.png
outputs.spec: One-color knockout: solid white HA mark on transparent (for dark backgrounds), hi-res PNG for dark-shirt print and dark wrap panels.

outputs.name: haulaway_horizontal_lockup.png
outputs.spec: Horizontal layout version, cropped to true content bounds, transparent hi-res PNG sized for vehicle door / business-card horizontal use.

outputs.name: haulaway_stacked_lockup.png
outputs.spec: Stacked/vertical (square-ish) layout version cropped tight, transparent hi-res PNG for car magnet and crew-shirt left-chest.

outputs.name: haulaway_trailerwrap_hero.png
outputs.spec: Creative bonus: high-impact lime HA mark on a solid black canvas (lime-on-black 'trailer wrap hero'), hi-res PNG meant to splash large across a trailer side. Lime achieved via color_overlay on the mark; black via change_background_color on the surrounding canvas.

outputs.name: haulaway_font_usage_guide.json
outputs.spec: Typography reference: recommended brand font family + heading/body pairing and usage notes from font_recommend, to accompany the kit.

outputs.name: haulaway_brand_board
outputs.spec: Firefly Board deep-link assembling all produced variations as the client presentation / brand sheet; board notes carry the black/lime/gray hex, CMYK and Pantone callouts and the font guidance.

connector_workflow.note: Ingest the client's supplied raster logo into CC storage and get a working asset reference/URI.
Finalize the upload to a stable presigned asset URI that the image_* tools can consume.
Correct the ~2.5° scan skew so the logo edges are true before tracing — straight paths trace cleaner.
Auto-tone the straightened logo to normalize exposure/contrast and recover clean black/white separation.
Boost only the lime-green channel so the brand green is punchy and clean-edged for the trace (other colors untouched).
Knock the near-white #F4F4F2 background to true transparent so the vectorize has no halo/fringe.
Vectorize the cleaned full lockup → MASTER print-ready SVG (deliverable; basis for AI/EPS/PNG).
Select the 'HaulAway' wordmark + tagline text by prompt so it can be removed to isolate the monogram.
Invert the wordmark selection so the kept region is just the 'HA' monogram mark.
Solid-fill the wordmark area with solid white (solid color only) leaving the isolated HA monogram.
Vectorize the isolated monogram → ICON-ONLY mark SVG (deliverable).
Apply a solid black color overlay to the isolated mark → one-color black-on-light knockout (deliverable).
Solid-fill the mark region pure white → white-on-dark one-color knockout for dark shirts/wraps (deliverable).
Crop the cleaned master to its true content bounds → tight horizontal layout lockup (deliverable).
Re-crop/resize to a square-ish frame → stacked/vertical layout version for magnet + left-chest (deliverable).
Creative bonus (step 1 of 2): apply a solid high-impact lime color overlay to the isolated mark → lime HA mark on transparent.
Creative bonus (step 2 of 2): set the transparent surrounding canvas to solid black → the final lime-on-black 'trailer wrap hero' (deliverable).
Recommend the brand font family + heading/body pairing referencing the wordmark style → typography usage guide (deliverable).

connector_workflow.inputs_from: haulaway_master_logo.svg, haulaway_icon_mark.svg, haulaway_mark_oneclr_black_on_light.png, haulaway_mark_oneclr_white_on_dark.png, haulaway_horizontal_lockup.png, haulaway_stacked_lockup.png, haulaway_trailerwrap_hero.png
connector_workflow.note: Assemble every produced variation into a Firefly Board presentation/brand sheet (board notes carry hex/CMYK/Pantone + font guidance) (deliverable).

chaining_note: Single connected chain, no orphan steps. The client's raw PNG is ingested (steps 1-2) to a stable asset URI, which feeds a linear cleanup pipeline: straighten (3) -> auto-tone (4) -> lime single-color saturation crisp (5) -> background removal to transparent (6). That cleaned transparent master (logo_clean_transparent) is the hub: it is vectorized into the MASTER svg (7); it feeds wordmark selection (8) whose mask is inverted (9) and the wordmark mask is used to solid-white-fill the wordmark away (10), yielding icon_only_raster; that isolated raster is vectorized into the ICON mark (11) and reused for the black-on-light knockout via black overlay (12), the white-on-dark knockout via white solid fill on the inverted keep-mask from 9 (13), and the lime trailer-wrap treatment (16). The same cleaned master is cropped to bounds for the horizontal lockup (14), which is re-cropped/resized into the stacked version (15). The lime mark (16) then gets a solid black canvas via change_background_color (17) to produce the final lime-on-black trailer-wrap hero. font_recommend (18) consumes the master svg wordmark style. create_firefly_board (19) consumes all seven produced visual deliverables. Every step after ingest reads a prior step's named output.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon and wide-mix: 18 connector calls, 15 distinct Adobe tools across three groups (asset ingest, the image_* raster/vector family, and font/board). Requires correct ordering (straighten + tone + background removal BEFORE vectorize for a clean trace), a non-trivial isolate-the-monogram sub-chain (select_by_prompt -> invert -> solid fill_area -> re-vectorize) exercising masking semantics exactly as the sheet allows, and disciplined reuse of one cleaned hub asset to spawn seven downstream variations. Stays strictly inside connector limits: no generative fill, no object removal, no compositing, no layout composition; image_fill_area as solid-color only; vector deliverables from image_vectorize (not an authored .ai); one-color knockouts via solid fill + color overlay. The hex/CMYK/Pantone color sheet and font guide are honestly delivered as font_recommend output + Firefly Board notes rather than a composed page. All steps are [C] headless-confirmed, so the task is fully autonomous-doable while remaining a demanding, faithful real-client brief."

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned capability present. image_fill_area is used strictly as a solid-color fill (solid white in step 10, solid white in step 13) — never to grow/square/extend the canvas or fill to transparency. Vector deliverables come only from image_vectorize (steps 7, 11), not from an authored .ai. change_background_color (new step 17) sets a solid background color on the existing canvas — it does not outpaint or enlarge. No generative fill/expand, no upscale/super-resolution, no text-to-image, no from-scratch art/logo, no AI object removal, no compositing, no layout composition. image_apply_lens_blur is not used. asset_preview/inline_preview not used to emit files. Every consumed input (haulaway_logo_raw.png and all derived intermediates/masks) is declared or produced by a prior step.
reverify.notes: All 19 steps are real Adobe connector tools tagged [C] headless. Chain is single and fully connected with no orphans; renumbering after the step-16 split is consistent (font_recommend=18, create_firefly_board=19). Stale banned-capability text was checked in full_brief, outputs[].spec, and chaining_note — the trailer-wrap description now names overlay + change_background_color; no 'generative/outpaint/upscale/from-scratch' phrasing remains. Brand naming is internally consistent (HaulAway wordmark + HA monogram + haulaway_* filenames); 'HaulAway' remains the fictional client brand from the source brief and does not collide with a real company. Counts consistent: 19 calls, 16 distinct tools.
